item.title: 多方计算入门（相对容易理解） | A (Relatively Easy To Understand) Primer on Multi-Party Computation
item.description:
区块链使用数字签名，它使用密钥对：私钥（ PK ）和公钥。 通过数字签名，任何拥有 PK 的人都可以签署交易以使用数字货币，并且假设 PK 具有访问权限，则可以控制智能合约。 因此，保护​​ PK 至关重要。 一些用户自己保护 PK 并接受被盗或丢失的风险。 其他人则信任在线钱包或交易所来保护他们的密钥。

在这两个选项中，用户都信任一个实体。 相比之下，阈值签名方案( TSS ) 需要至少两个合作参与者的阈值才能生成签名。

多方计算( MPC ) 和多重签名或多重签名都提供这些功能。 生成的 MPC 签名可与单签名者签名互换。 然而，通过多重签名，每一方都有一个 PK。 这种微小的差异会显着影响区块链的成本、速度和可用性。 具体而言，对于可用性，每个区块链都需要本地或通过智能合约的多重签名支持。 支持这些增加了每个新区块链的开发工作。

与确保通信的安全性和完整性以及对手是外部方的传统加密任务不同，该模型可以保护党员彼此的隐私。

输入隐私：不能从协议执行期间发送的消息中推断出有关各方持有的私有数据的任何信息。

  • 正确性：任何愿意共享信息或偏离协议执行指令的合谋方子集都不应强迫诚实方输出不正确的结果。 这个目标有两种形式：要么保证合法方计算正确的输出（“稳健”协议），要么在发现错误时中止（“带有中止”的协议）。

    • 阈值签名算法具有三个阶段，

    1. 生成密钥对，将 PK 拆分为多个秘密共享，并在各方之间分配这些共享。 分布式密钥生成确保每个节点只学习其份额； 完整的密钥永远不会在一个地方组装或提供。
    2. 收集门槛吨吨 使用标准签名验证算法验证签名。

    一个天真的例子

    三个同事 Alice、Bob 和 Carol 想知道他们的平均工资，但不透露他们的工资。 那么，他们希望联合计算的函数是， 一个五小号一个小号乙小号平均（S_A，S_B，S_C） 如果他们有一个值得信赖的朋友戴夫，他们知道他可以保守秘密，他们每个人都可以告诉他他们的薪水。 戴夫可以计算平均值并告诉所有人。 爱丽丝选择一个只有她知道的随机数并将其添加到她的工资中。 然后她安全地将结果传递给 Bob。 各方只能了解他们的输入和输出，如果信任他们诚实的朋友戴夫，他们会学到的确切信息。

    简而言之，这就是 MPC。 在实践中，由于各种原因（例如面对合谋和恶意行为者的鲁棒性），它要复杂得多。

    示例用例

    • 分布式投票
    • 私人投标和拍卖
    • 共享签名或解密功能
    • 私人信息检索（允许用户从拥有数据库的服务器中检索项目，而无需透露检索到的项目）

    密钥对生成与秘密共享

    如果一个实体控制客户端的 PK，则系统不是去中心化的。 然后节点使用 MPC 协议一起工作以执行必要的计算。

    秘密共享可防止节点故障。 秘密共享是一种在群体中分发秘密的方法，这样任何人都不会掌握任何关于秘密的可理解信息。 然而，足够多的人可以结合他们的份额来重建秘密。 任何一组吨吨 吨吨 这样的系统称为吨考虑秘密共享方案，其中磷一世是公共非对称加密密钥，并且问一世是它们对应的PK。 每一方磷磷磷问在该方案中，任何拥有 PK 1 的一方都可以移除外层加密，拥有 PK 1 和 2 的一方可以移除第一层和第二层，以此类推。 聚会少于这个问题目前在计算上是不可行的。

    与上述方案相反，可以通过任何吨吨 基于拉格朗日插值定理，基本思想是吨吨 吨例如，两个点足以定义一条线，三个足以表示抛物线，等等。 方法是创建一个多项式吨随机选择剩余的系数。 然后，找到曲线上的n个点，并给每一方一个。 至少当吨吨 吨如果不清楚， 请参阅此博客文章以获取替代解释。

    每个 MPC 节点只接收一个 PK 共享，并使用它来构建部分交易签名以广播到网络。 即使某个节点下线、失去 PK 访问权限或存在恶意，仍然可以从剩余节点重建完整的交易。



    原文:

    Blockchains use digital signatures, which use key pairs: a private key (PK) and a public key. Through digital signatures, any person with a PK can sign transactions to spend digital currencies and, assuming the PK has access, control smart contracts. Therefore, it is crucial to safeguard PKs. Some users safeguard PKs themselves and accept the risk of theft or loss. Others trust online wallets or exchanges to protect their keys.

    In both these options, the user puts all their trust in a single entity. In contrast, threshold signature schemes (TSS) require a threshold of at least two cooperating participants to produce a signature.

    Both multi-party computation (MPC) and multi-signature, or multi-sig, offer these capabilities. The resulting MPC signature is interchangeable with a single-signer signature. However, with multi-sig, each party has a PK. This slight difference significantly impacts the cost, speed, and availability on blockchains. For availability specifically, each blockchain requires multi-sig support either natively or via smart contracts. Supporting these increases development efforts for each new blockchain.

    MPC overview

    MPC is a sub-field of cryptography allowing parties to jointly compute a function over their inputs while keeping those inputs private. Unlike traditional cryptographic tasks that assure the security and integrity of communication and where the adversary is an outside party, this model protects party member privacy from each other.

    The fundamental goals of an MPC protocol are,

    1. Input privacy: one cannot infer any information about the private data held by the parties from the messages sent during protocol execution.
    2. Correctness: any subset of colluding parties willing to share information or deviate from protocol execution instructions should not be able to force honest parties to output an incorrect result. This goal comes in two flavors: either the legitimate parties are guaranteed to compute the correct output (a "robust" protocol), or they abort if they find an error (a protocol "with abort").

    A threshold signing algorithm has three phases,

    1. Generate the key pair, split the PK into multiple secret shares, and distribute these shares between parties. Distributed key generation ensures each node only learns its share; the full key is never assembled or available in one place.
    2. Gather a threshold of ttt parties and run an MPC protocol to sign the transaction.
    3. Verify the signature using standard signature verification algorithms.

    A naive example

    Three coworkers, Alice, Bob, and Carol, want to know their average salary without disclosing their salaries. The function they wish to compute jointly, then, is, AVG(SA,SB,SC)AVG(S_A, S_B, S_C)AVG(SA​,SB​,SC​) .

    If they had a trusted friend, Dave, who they knew could keep a secret, they could each tell him their salary. Dave could compute the average and tell all of them. The goal of MPC is to design a protocol where, by exchanging messages only with each other, Alice, Bob, and Carol can still learn the average without revealing who makes what and without relying on Dave.

    Alice picks a random number known only to her and adds it to her salary. She then securely passes the result to Bob. Bob adds his salary to the first result and gives the result to Carol. Carol does the same and returns the result to Alice. Alice then subtracts the random number from the total and calculates the average salary. The parties can only learn their input and the output, the exact information they would learn if trusting their honest friend, Dave.

    That's MPC in a nutshell. In practice, it's far more complex for various reasons (such as robustness in the face of collusion and malicious actors).

    Example use cases

    • Distributed voting
    • Private bidding and auctions
    • Sharing of signature or decryption functions
    • Private information retrieval (allowing a user to retrieve an item from a server in possession of a database without revealing which item was retrieved)

    Key pair generation with secret sharing

    A system is not decentralized if one entity controls a client's PK. MPC nodes, or nodes for short, own only one piece of a PK, known as a key share. The nodes then work together using an MPC protocol to perform necessary computations.

    Secret sharing protects against node failures. Secret sharing is a method for distributing a secret among a group so that no individual holds any intelligible information about the secret. However, a sufficient number of individuals may combine their shares to reconstruct the secret. Any group of ttt **(for *threshold*) or more can together reconstruct the secret, but no group of fewer than ttt can. Such a system is called a (t,n)(t, n)(t,n) -threshold scheme.

    Consider the secret sharing scheme where XXX is the secret to be shared, PiP_iPi​ are public asymmetric encryption keys, and QiQ_iQi​ are their corresponding PKs. Each party JJJ is provided with {P1(P2(...(PN(X)))),Qj}\{P_1 (P_2 (...(P_N (X )))), Q_j\}{P1​(P2​(...(PN​(X)))),Qj​} . In this scheme, any party with PK one can remove the outer layer of encryption, a party with PKs one and two can remove the first and second layers, and so on. A party with fewer than NNN keys can never fully reach the secret XXX without first needing to decrypt a public-key-encrypted blob for which they do not have the corresponding PK. This problem is currently computationally infeasible.

    Shamir's Secret Sharing is a popular scheme. In contrast to the scheme above, one may recover the secret with any ttt out of nnn shares instead of requiring all nnn . Based on the Lagrange interpolation theorem, the essential idea is that ttt points are enough to uniquely determine a polynomial of degree less than or equal to t−1t - 1t−1 . For instance, two points are sufficient to define a line, three are enough to represent a parabola, and so on. The method is to create a polynomial of degree t−1t - 1t−1 with the secret as the first coefficient; pick the remaining coefficients randomly. Then, find nnn points on the curve and give one to each party. When at least ttt out of the nnn parties reveal their points, there is sufficient information to fit a (t−1)(t-1)(t−1) th degree polynomial, with the first coefficient being the secret. If this is unclear, see this blog post for an alternate explanation.

    Each MPC node receives only one PK share and uses it to construct partial transaction signatures to broadcast to the network. Even if a node is offline, loses PK access, or is malicious, it is still possible to reconstruct the complete transaction from the remaining nodes.
item.author: therockstorm

item.title: 使用 bcryptjs 散列和比较散列密码（同步方法） | Hashing and comparing hashed passwords with bcryptjs (synchronous method)
item.description:
您的密码不会以纯文本形式存储在您注册的任何网站上。 这样做的原因是，如果有人可以访问数据库，他们会发现很难知道他们在数据库中看到的任何帐户的密码。

散列是将纯文本转换为另一个值的过程。 生成的散列可以与纯文本进行比较，但是，当对纯文本进行散列时，它不能转换回纯文本。 此外，一个好的散列算法应该能够生成唯一的散列，即使对相同的平原进行散列也是如此。 这意味着如果我对“舒适”之类的词进行两次哈希处理，例如，每次哈希处理时它都应该生成唯一值。

什么是 bcryptjs？

设置 bcryptjs

创建一个文件夹，将其命名为“bcrypt-tutorial”，在该文件夹内，打开终端并输入“npm init -y”创建项目，然后输入“touch index.js”创建要使用的 index.js 文件编写我们的代码。
在此之后，您使用您最喜欢的代码编辑器打开“bcrypt-tutorial”，在本文中我将使用 VS Code。

然后，使用命令安装 bcryptjs

到目前为止，您已经为项目创建了一个文件夹，创建了一个项目，以便可以从该文件夹中管理您的依赖项，并且您已经安装了“bcryptjs”，做得很好。

如何散列密码

假设我们要散列的密码是“舒适的”。

在需要/导入 bcryptjs 后创建的 index.js 文件中，键入以下内容：

 const bcrypt = require("bcryptjs"); const password = "comfortable"; const salt = bcrypt.genSaltSync(10); const hashedPassword = bcrypt.hashSync(password, salt); console.log(hashedPassword);

让我解释一下到目前为止我们编写的代码行：

常量 bcrypt = 要求（“bcryptjs”）；

这行代码导入要在 indes.js 文件中使用的 bcryptjs 包。

常量密码=“舒适”；

我们要散列的密码。

常量盐 = bcrypt.genSaltSync(10);

这是生成的盐，用于对纯密码进行哈希处理。 散列盐可以定义为散列函数的成本因子——将其加一将使计算散列所需的时间加倍。 作为参数传递的“10”意味着哈希函数将花费 10 倍的时间来计算哈希。

这行代码实际上使用“bcrypt.genSaltSynce()”生成的盐对密码进行哈希处理。

这行代码显示了对密码进行哈希处理的结果。

这是我得到的结果$2a$10$qADzCMgxKJjE7gLNdh0M6.cWWYyyDDHUCQWMAXdk87pFuOrkCSpQO

接下来，我想你会想知道如何将散列密码与普通密码进行比较，让我们看看如何做到这一点。

如何比较普通密码和散列密码。

假设您想将原始密码（“舒适”）与散列密码进行比较，看看它们是否匹配，这里是如何做到的。

 const bcrypt = require("bcryptjs"); const password = "comfortable"; // The value of the he password I hashed // Your's will be different const hashedPassword = "$2a$10$qADzCMgxKJjE7gLNdh0M6.cWWYyyDDHUCQWMAXdk87pFuOrkCSpQO"; const passwordsMatch = bcrypt.compareSync(password, hashedPassword); console.log(passwordsMatch); if(passwordsMatch) { console.log("Passwords match"); } else { console.log("Passwords don't match"); }

让我解释一下我们到目前为止所写的内容。

被散列的密码的值。

我们使用这行代码将纯密码与散列密码进行比较，看看它们是否匹配。 如果密码匹配，这将返回布尔值 true，如果密码不匹配，则返回 false。

这行代码采用存储为 passwordsMatch 的布尔值，并使用它向用户打印密码是否匹配的消息。

我得到的结果：

结论

在您的服务器上设置注册路由时可以使用此方法。 在保存任何用户的密码之前，请始终确保对其进行哈希处理。



原文:

Your passwords are not stored as plain text on any website you've signed up for. The reason for this is that if someone gets access to the database, they will find it difficult to know the passwords of any account they have seen on the database.

Hashing is the process of converting plain text into another value. The hash generated can be compared with the plain text, but, when plain text is hashed it can't be converted back to plain text. Also, a good hashing algorithm is supposed to generate unique hashes even if the same plain is hashed. This means if I hash a word like "comfortable" twice, for example, it should generate unique values each time I hash it.

What is bcryptjs?

Bcryptjs is a library that can be used to hash passwords before storing and also comparing plain text passwords with hashed passwords to see if they match.

Setup bcryptjs

Create a folder, name it "bcrypt-tutorial", inside the folder, open a terminal and type "npm init -y" to create a project, then type "touch index.js" to create an index.js file to be used to write our code.
After this, you open "bcrypt-tutorial" with your favorite code editor, in this article I'll use VS Code.

Then, you install bcryptjs by using the command

So far, you've created a folder for the project, created a project so that your dependencies can be managed from within the folder and you've installed "bcryptjs", well done.

How to hash a password

Let's say the password we want to hash is "comfortable".

Inside the index.js file you created after requiring/importing bcryptjs, type the following:

const bcrypt = require("bcryptjs");

const password = "comfortable";

const salt = bcrypt.genSaltSync(10);

const hashedPassword = bcrypt.hashSync(password, salt);

console.log(hashedPassword);

Let me explain the lines of code we have written so far:

const bcrypt = require("bcryptjs");

This line of code imports the bcryptjs package to be used inside the indes.js file.

const password = "comfortable";

The password we want to hash.

const salt = bcrypt.genSaltSync(10);

This is the salt generated to be used to hash the plain password. A hash salt can be defined as the cost factor for the hash function - incrementing it by one will double the time taken to calculate the hash. The "10" passed as an argument means the hash function will take 10 times the time to calculate the hash.

const hashedPassword = bcrypt.hashSync(password, salt);

This line of code actually hashes the password using the salt generated using "bcrypt.genSaltSynce()".

console.log(hashedPassword);

This line of code shows the result of hashing the password.

This is the result i got $2a$10$qADzCMgxKJjE7gLNdh0M6.cWWYyyDDHUCQWMAXdk87pFuOrkCSpQO

Up next, I guess you will want to know how to compare the hashed password with the plain password, let's see how to do that.

How to compare a plain password with a hashed password.

Let's say you want to compare the original password (which is "comfortable") with the hashed password to see if they match, here is how to do it.

const bcrypt = require("bcryptjs");

const password = "comfortable";

// The value of the he password I hashed
// Your's will be different
const hashedPassword = "$2a$10$qADzCMgxKJjE7gLNdh0M6.cWWYyyDDHUCQWMAXdk87pFuOrkCSpQO";

const passwordsMatch = bcrypt.compareSync(password, hashedPassword);

console.log(passwordsMatch);

if(passwordsMatch) {
    console.log("Passwords match");
} else {
    console.log("Passwords don't match");
}

Let me explain what we've written so far.

const hashedPassword = "...";

The value of the password that was hashed.

const passwordsMatch = bcrypt.compareSync(password, hashedPassword);

We use this line of code to compare the plain password with the hashed password to see if they match. This will return a Boolean of true if the passwords match and false if the passwords don't match.

if(passwordsMatch) { console.log("Passwords match"); } else { console.log("Passwords don't match");

}


This line of code takes the Boolean value stored as passwordsMatch and uses it to print a message to the user if the passwords match or not.

The result I got:

Conclusion

This method can be used when setting up a registration route on your server. Before saving any user's password, always make sure you hash it.
item.author: victuk

item.title: GraphQL : 像 SQL 一样处理 REST API | GraphQL : Deal REST API like SQL
item.description:
作为后端开发人员，您是否曾经遇到过这些问题，前端需要对字段名称进行大量更改，或者 API 响应中缺少字段，或者您发送的字段超过 UI 所需的字段，而前端对此很生气这个，或者你有多个消费者，每个消费者都希望得到他想要的响应。

另一方面，作为前端开发人员，您是否遇到过 API 响应字段名称与您的对象不匹配，您需要在不同的 UI 组件中使用相同的资源，并在后端处理它们之前被阻止，后端在将它们发送到之前未格式化日期和字符串您或格式与您的 UI 不匹配。

如果我们可以将 REST API 作为某种 SQL 来处理呢：只选择需要的内容，根据需要格式化响应并根据需要操作响应。

这种魔法是如何发生的？

您必须执行设置步骤，以便 GraphQL 引擎可以对其进行操作，首先您必须构建graphql 模式来定义您提供的操作，例如 getAllTransactions() 、 createTransaction() 以及这些操作需要哪些参数以及它们返回什么，以及此设置必须驻留在**.graphql * 扩展中，具体语法如下

定义架构后，您必须在架构中的操作和在这些操作调用期间必须完成的实际逻辑之间进行链接，操作和逻辑之间的链接是通过DataFetcher组件完成的，因为 Graphql 引擎不执行逻辑而是只查看您的架构和请求者请求什么，然后在它们之间进行匹配。

操作和逻辑之间的 GraphQL 链接必须如何完成？

当您启动应用程序时，GraphQL 引擎将提取您的模式文件并解析它们，如果有任何语法错误，它将崩溃。 还DataFetcher用于链接操作名称和应该执行的服务方法，然后此设置转到 SchemaGenerator 以生成GraphQLSchema并公开 GraphQL API，如下所示

现在 GraphQL API 负责

  1. 接收请求者的请求。
  2. 提取操作名称并与模式文件中定义的名称之一匹配。
  3. 与 DataFetcher 中的操作相关联的消防方法。
  4. 从服务方法获取结果，过滤请求中需要的字段，并在数据上应用所需的任何格式。
  5. 将响应返回给请求者。

请求 - 响应示例

幸运的是，现在存在许多帮助程序库，您的设置不会超出定义架构文件和实现提供业务逻辑的接口并让魔术开始。

没有什么是完美的！

让我们来看看 GraphQL 的优缺点

优点

  • 当 UI 继续更改时，限制更改后端 API 的麻烦。
  • 使用相同的后端服务支持具有不同要求的多个 API 使用者。
  • 一个 GraphQL API 可以根据您的模式文件处理多个操作，并且不必担心合同只需给消费者一个 URL，您就完成了。
  • 用于提供 GraphQL UI 的帮助程序库，消费者可以浏览您的架构并测试操作。

缺点

  • 对 GraphQL 引擎产生性能影响是可行的，因此您必须明智地使用它。
  • 不能依赖 HTTP 错误代码，因为 GraphQL 总是发送 200/Ok 即使有错误，但它会在这种情况下为您提供错误文件。
  • 您必须为 GraphQL 配置特殊的异常处理程序层，因为请求现在与后端服务的其余部分采用不同的路径。

资源

  • 如果你喜欢这篇文章，那么你可以用一杯咖啡来支持这样的内容，在此先感谢。



原文:

Have you ever faced these problems, as a back-end developer which front-end requires a lot of changes regarding field names, or field is missing in API response, or you are sending more fields than UI requires and front-end got mad about this, or you have multiple consumers and each consumer want the response as he wishes.

On other hand as front-end developer have you faced that API response field names not match your object, you need same resource in different UI components and blocked until back-end handle them, back-end not formatting dates and strings before sending them to you or formatting don't match with your UI.

What about if we can deal with REST APIs as some sort of SQL : selecting what needed only, formatting the response as we want and manipulate response with our needs.

GraphQL in your service

Graph Query Language is a manipulation language for APIs made by Facebook(meta) to solve problems of continuous UI changing with multiple devices and requirements and propagation of this changing impact to back-end APIs to accommodate with, so let back-end build one API and each consumer can pull what he needs from it, manipulate field name as he needs and formatting the values as per his need.

How this magic happens?

You have to do setup steps so that GraphQL engine can work upon it , At first you have to build graphql schema that define what operations you provide like getAllTransactions() , createTransaction() with what parameters these operations takes and what are they return , and this setup have to be resident in **.graphql* extension with specific syntax as below

After defining your schema you have to link between operations in schema and actual logic that have to be done during these operations call, Linking between operations and logic is done through DataFetcher component, as Graphql engine not performing logic instead it just look at your schema and what requester requests, Then match between them.

How GraphQL link between operation and logic have to be done?

When you start your application GraphQL engine will pull your schema files and parse them, if there any syntax error it will crash. also DataFetcher used to link between operation name and service method that should be executed with , Then this setup goes to SchemaGenerator to generate GraphQLSchema and expose GraphQL API as below

Now GraphQL API responsible for

  1. Receiving the request from requester.
  2. Extract operation name and match with one of defined in schema files.
  3. Fire service method that wired with operation in DataFetcher.
  4. Get result from service method, filter what fields needed in Request and applying any format needed upon data.
  5. Return response to requester.

request - response sample

Fortunately, a lot of helper libraries now exists, and your setup will not go beyond define your schema files and implementing an interface that provide your business logic and let the magic begins.

Nothing is perfect!

let's taking about GraphQL pros and cons

Pros

  • Limiting the hassle of change back-end APIs when continues changes of UI.
  • Supporting multiple API consumers with different requirements with same back-end service.
  • One GraphQL API can handle multiple operations as per your schema files and don't worry about contract just give consumers one URL and your are done.
  • Helper libraries for providing GraphQL UI that consumer can browse your schema and test the operation.

Cons

  • Having performance impact for GraphQL engine works so you have to use it wisely.
  • Can't depend on HTTP error codes as GraphQL always send 200/Ok even with errors but it provide you with error filed in that case.
  • You have to configure special exceptional handler layer for GraphQL as the request now takes different path from rest of your back-end service.

Resources

  • GraphQL Documentation

  • GraphQL Video

IF YOU LIKED THE POST, THEN YOU CAN SUPPPORT SUCH CONTENT WITH A CUP OF COFFEE, THANKS IN ADVANCE.
item.author: smuhammed

item.title: 培养深厚的工作文化的重要性 | The Importance of Cultivating a Deep Work Culture
item.description:
受保护的“创客”、“流动”或“深度工作”时间对生产力很重要。 大公司很难围绕会议改变他们的文化，这对初创公司来说不是。 实施深厚的工作文化并随着公司的发展对其进行培养，为他们提供了强大的竞争优势：提高了每位员工的生产力。 这意味着为员工人数相对较少的客户提供高价值。 反过来，低员工人数会使沟通开销保持在较低水平，从而形成积极的飞轮。

研究

有两种类型的日程安排，我称之为经理日程安排和制造商日程安排。 经理的日程安排是给老板的。 它体现在传统的约会簿中，每天以一小时为间隔。 如果需要，您可以为单个任务安排几个小时，但默认情况下，您每小时都会更改您正在做的事情。

当你以这种方式使用时间时，与某人会面只是一个实际问题。 在您的日程安排中找到一个空缺的位置，预订它们，然后您就完成了。

最有权势的人都在经理的日程表上。 这是命令的时间表。 但是还有另一种使用时间的方式，这种方式在程序员和作家等创造事物的人中很常见。 他们通常更喜欢以至少半天为单位使用时间。 你不能以一小时为单位写好或编程。 这几乎没有足够的时间开始。

当您按照制造商的时间表进行操作时，会议就是一场灾难。 一次会议可能会浪费一整个下午，把它分成两块，每块都太小了，不能做任何困难的事情。另外，你必须记得去开会。 对于经理日程安排上的人来说，这没问题。 下一个小时总会有事情发生； 唯一的问题是什么。 但是，当制造商日程上的某个人开会时，他们必须考虑一下。

我发现一次会议有时会影响一整天。 一次会议通常至少要花半天时间，早上或下午就分开。 但除此之外，有时还会产生级联效应。 如果我知道下午会被打破，我不太可能在早上开始一些雄心勃勃的事情。 我知道这听起来可能过于敏感，但如果您是制造商，请考虑您自己的情况。 想到一整天都可以自由工作，没有任何约会，您的精神是否会振奋？ 嗯，这意味着当你不这样做时，你的精神就会相应地沮丧。 雄心勃勃的项目在定义上接近你的能力极限。 士气的小幅下降足以杀死他们。

每种类型的时间表本身都可以正常工作。 当他们相遇时，问题就出现了。 由于大多数有权势的人都按照经理的日程安排工作，因此如果他们愿意，他们可以让每个人都以他们的频率产生共鸣。 但更聪明的人会克制自己，如果他们知道为他们工作的一些人需要很长时间
大块的时间来工作。

我们这些在制造商日程上的人愿意妥协。 我们知道我们必须举行一些会议。 我们对经理日程安排上的所有要求是他们了解成本。

从 Cal Newport 对Deep Work的总结中，

深度工作是一种专注于认知要求高的任务而不会分心的能力。 这是一项技能，可以让您快速掌握复杂的信息，并在更短的时间内产生更好的结果。 深入的工作将使您在所做的事情上做得更好，并提供来自工艺的真正成就感。 简而言之，深度工作就像我们竞争日益激烈的 21 世纪经济中的超级大国。 然而，大多数人已经失去了深入研究的能力——他们每天都在疯狂的电子邮件和社交媒体中度过，甚至没有意识到有更好的方法。

摘自 Cal Newport 对一个没有电子邮件的世界的总结（一个不幸的标题，但他在书中也明确并反复提到了 Slack），

现代知识工作者不断地交流。 他们的日子是由不断涌入的消息和来回的数字对话定义的——一种持续的、焦虑的喋喋不休的状态，没有人可以断开连接，因此没有人有足够的认知带宽来执行实质性的工作。 曾经有一段时间，电子邮件之类的工具感觉很先进，但对现有证据的彻底审查表明，他们帮助创建的“过度活跃的蜂巢思维”工作流程已成为生产力灾难，降低了盈利能力，甚至可能减缓整体经济增长。 同样令人担忧的是，这让我们很痛苦。 人类根本无法进行持续的数字通信。

我们已经习惯了收件箱驱动的工作日，以至于很难想象其他选择。 但它们确实存在。 凭借多年的调查报告，作家兼计算机科学教授 Cal Newport 提出了我们当前的工作方法被打破的案例，然后列出了一系列原则和具体说明来解决它。 在一个没有电子邮件的世界中，他主张建立一个工作场所，在这个工作场所中，明确的流程——而不是随意的信息——定义了如何识别、分配和审查任务。 每个人做的事情更少（但做得更好），积极的支持投资减少了不断增加的管理任务负担。 最重要的是，重要的沟通得到了简化，收件箱和聊天频道不再是工作展开的核心。

最后，从 Jason Fried 和 David Heinemeier Hansson的《工作中不必疯狂》的总结中，

冷静是作为最后手段的会议。 冷静是语境交流。 平静是更多的独立，更少的相互依赖。

外卖

初创公司可以通过以下方式培养深度工作，

  • 与同步 Slack 相比，支持工单跟踪器（例如 Trello、Linear、JIRA）中的异步通信。
    • 票务跟踪器应包含特定功能和项目的所有上下文（包括指向任何文档的链接和有关该主题的会议记录），而不必在 Slack 中搜索零碎的对话。
    • 这样，拿起一个新故事就可以为您提供完成它所需的一切。 这可能需要更详细地分解故事，以减少实施问题。
  • 使用顺时针或类似方式来帮助安排会议。
    • 它考虑到每个人的日程安排，尽可能将会议分组，为深入工作留下不间断的时间块。
    • 有一个选项可以阻止日历上的“专注时间”，它与 Slack 集成以在这些时间打开“请勿打扰”，这样您就不会被Slack 通知噪音分心。
    • 理解这可能意味着延迟的 Slack 响应。 那应该没问题！ 让人们继续深入工作，并在他们出现时做出回应。
  • 至少有一个，最好是两个“无会议日”，供制造者和可选的管理者使用。
    • 这几天没有安排节奏会议
    • 松弛站立，而不是面对面或通过视频聊天
    • 为阻止者召开临时会议是可以的，但请尝试准备多张票，以便在遇到困难时可以转移到另一张票上。
    • 作为替代“无会议日”的另一种选择，设置双模式日，早上用于会议，下午用于深度工作（反之亦然）。
  • 会议邀请必须包括 GAP：目标、议程和会前准备。 如果他们不这样做，您可以跳过会议——没有 GAP，无需参加。

如果公司接受这种工作方式，投资回报率可能会很大。 这是一种思维方式的转变。 但就我个人而言，我最好的工作是在我忘记时间，忘记吃东西，然后从另一边出来的时候完成了该死的事情。 想象一下，如果整个公司都可以优化他们的工作时间以尽可能多地利用这些时间。 你的竞争对手有麻烦了。

莱昂纳多·蒙蒂尼—— 一旦暂停，therockstorm 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，therockstorm 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，therockstorm 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 therockstorm 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 therockstorm 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 therockstorm 将恢复他们帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Protected "maker", "flow", or "deep work" time is important to productivity. It's difficult for larger companies to change their culture around meetings, it's not for startups. Implementing a deep work culture and fostering it as companies grow gives them a strong competitive advantage: increased per-employee productivity. This means delivering high value to customers with relatively low headcount. A low headcount, in turn, keeps communication overhead low, creating a positive flywheel.

Research

Snippets from Maker's Schedule, Manager's Schedule by Paul Graham,

There are two types of schedule, which I'll call the manager's schedule and the maker's schedule. The manager's schedule is for bosses. It's embodied in the traditional appointment book, with each day cut into one hour intervals. You can block off several hours for a single task if you need to, but by default you change what you're doing every hour.

When you use time that way, it's merely a practical problem to meet with someone. Find an open slot in your schedule, book them, and you're done.

Most powerful people are on the manager's schedule. It's the schedule of command. But there's another way of using time that's common among people who make things, like programmers and writers. They generally prefer to use time in units of half a day at least. You can't write or program well in units of an hour. That's barely enough time to get started.

When you're operating on the maker's schedule, meetings are a disaster. A single meeting can blow a whole afternoon, by breaking it into two pieces each too small to do anything hard in. Plus you have to remember to go to the meeting. That's no problem for someone on the manager's schedule. There's always something coming on the next hour; the only question is what. But when someone on the maker's schedule has a meeting, they have to think about it.

I find one meeting can sometimes affect a whole day. A meeting commonly blows at least half a day, by breaking up a morning or afternoon. But in addition there's sometimes a cascading effect. If I know the afternoon is going to be broken up, I'm slightly less likely to start something ambitious in the morning. I know this may sound oversensitive, but if you're a maker, think of your own case. Don't your spirits rise at the thought of having an entire day free to work, with no appointments at all? Well, that means your spirits are correspondingly depressed when you don't. And ambitious projects are by definition close to the limits of your capacity. A small decrease in morale is enough to kill them off.

Each type of schedule works fine by itself. Problems arise when they meet. Since most powerful people operate on the manager's schedule, they're in a position to make everyone resonate at their frequency if they want to. But the smarter ones restrain themselves, if they know that some of the people working for them need long
chunks of time to work in.

Those of us on the maker's schedule are willing to compromise. We know we have to have some number of meetings. All we ask from those on the manager's schedule is that they understand the cost.

From a summary of Deep Work by Cal Newport,

Deep work is the ability to focus without distraction on a cognitively demanding task. It's a skill that allows you to quickly master complicated information and produce better results in less time. Deep work will make you better at what you do and provide the sense of true fulfillment that comes from craftsmanship. In short, deep work is like a super power in our increasingly competitive twenty-first century economy. And yet, most people have lost the ability to go deep—spending their days instead in a frantic blur of e-mail and social media, not even realizing there's a better way.

From a summary of A World Without Email by Cal Newport (an unfortunate title, but he specifically and repeatedly mentions Slack in the book as well),

Modern knowledge workers communicate constantly. Their days are defined by a relentless barrage of incoming messages and back-and-forth digital conversations–a state of constant, anxious chatter in which nobody can disconnect, and so nobody has the cognitive bandwidth to perform substantive work. There was a time when tools like email felt cutting edge, but a thorough review of current evidence reveals that the "hyperactive hive mind" workflow they helped create has become a productivity disaster, reducing profitability and perhaps even slowing overall economic growth. Equally worrisome, it makes us miserable. Humans are simply not wired for constant digital communication.

We have become so used to an inbox-driven workday that it's hard to imagine alternatives. But they do exist. Drawing on years of investigative reporting, author and computer science professor Cal Newport makes the case that our current approach to work is broken, then lays out a series of principles and concrete instructions for fixing it. In A World without Email, he argues for a workplace in which clear processes–not haphazard messaging–define how tasks are identified, assigned and reviewed. Each person works on fewer things (but does them better), and aggressive investment in support reduces the ever-increasing burden of administrative tasks. Above all else, important communication is streamlined, and inboxes and chat channels are no longer central to how work unfolds.

Finally, from a summary of It Doesn't Have to be Crazy at Work by Jason Fried and David Heinemeier Hansson,

Calm is meetings as a last resort. Calm is contextual communication. Calm is asynchronous first, real-time second.

Calm is more independence, less interdependence.

Takeaways

Startups can cultivate deep work in the following ways,

  • Favor asynchronous communication in ticket trackers (e.g., Trello, Linear, JIRA) over synchronous Slack.
    • Ticket trackers should contain all context for specific features and projects (including links to any documents and meeting notes on the subject) rather than having to search for piecemeal conversations in Slack.
    • In this way, picking up a new story gives you everything you need to know to accomplish it. This may require breaking stories down in more detail so there are fewer implementation questions.
  • Use Clockwise or similar to aid in meeting scheduling.
    • It takes each person's calendar into consideration to group meetings into chunks as much as possible, leaving uninterrupted blocks of time for deep work.
    • There is an option to block off "Focus Time" on your calendar and it integrates with Slack to turn on "Do not disturb" during these times so you don't get distracted by Slack notification noise.
    • Understand this may mean delayed Slack responses. That should be okay! Allow people to remain in deep work and respond when they've come up for air.
  • At least one, ideally two "No meeting days" for makers and optionally managers.
    • No cadence meetings scheduled on these days
    • Slack standup instead of in-person or over video chat
    • Impromptu meetings for blockers are okay, but try to have multiple tickets to work on so you can move onto another if you get stuck.
    • As another option in place of "No meeting days", have bi-modal days where the mornings are for meetings and the afternoons are for deep work (or vice versa).
  • Meeting invites must include GAP: goals, an agenda, and pre-meeting preparation. If they don't, you can skip the meeting — no GAP, no need to attend.

If the company buys into this way of working, the ROI is potentially large. It's a mindset shift. But for me personally, my best work is done when I lose all track of time, forget to eat, and come out on the other side with shit fucking done. Imagine if an entire company could optimize their days to get as much of that time as possible. Your competition is in trouble.

Leonardo Montini - Oct 14
